title: Error trying to reuse blob at destination unauthorized: authentication required
body:
Hi all, thanks for the great work on skopeo and the related codebase.

Executing an image copy *sometimes* results in an authentication failure during the blob existence check.  We see this consistently for certain layers in certain images when copying several images with multiple `skopeo copy` commands, each for a different image:tag.  It's as if certain layers are not accessible, although this is not the case.

Manifest format auto-detection is disabled with `--format=v2s1` due to
```
# example of manifest format failure (does not occur on the problem image below)
Error writing manifest: Error uploading manifest 5bd6249c to dest.example.com/orgrepo/myotherimage: manifest invalid: manifest invalid
```

Versions:
* source `Harbor v1.10.2`
* destination `Quay v3.3.0`
* `skopeo version 1.1.1`

Debug output for `skopeo copy --src-creds=user:pass --dest-creds=user:pass --format=v2s1 docker://src.example.com/c107/ping-federate-upgrade:sha-92dc3742 docker://dest.example.com/orgrepo/myimage:sha-92dc3742`
(names have been changed)

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

I can’t see anything unexpected happening in the log; despite the “reuse” wording, this HTTP request is done on every single layer upload (and the request is a simple presence check with only the layer digest as a parameter), so if this were a code bug, I would expect it to fail for _all_ pushes, or at least all pushes to that server.

My best guess is that this is some kind of server-side permission issue (intentionally placed ACLs, or something in the backing storage?), or maybe the server rate limiting or applying some other client-invisible policy. The server is returning no additional details in the HTTP response body, so the root cause will probably have to be diagnosed server-side.

We changed to use `skopeo sync` and are not seeing this issue.
Thanks for taking the time to provide feedback.

Long dead issue here... but same with me... I gave up on "copy" due to this issue but "sync" works.

In this case artifactory -> ecr

I mean, `skopeo copy` and `skopeo sync` is literally the same code. The only difference I could see is a no-op sync, in which case it might not be necessary to have write permissions to the destination (but it would fail without those write permissions if it did have to sync a new image).

By far my primary guess would be that the credentials provided are not valid. Alternatively, note that the way `skopeo sync` maps registry names makes `skopeo copy a b` and `skopeo sync a b` not do the same copy operation.
Credentials in our case are most certainly valid and sync succeeds where copy fails.  There was no resolution for copy and we continue to have this issue when using `skopeo copy` today.

I faced the same issue, the credentials are valid. Changing the method from copy to sync fix the issue. (I think the issue is caused somehow related to image blobs that may already exist in the target registry, but the owner is another user. But this is just a wild guess.)

@hobti01 Did you find a solution to sync the image from the repository using other image names? The copy command is able to copy the image using a different name in the target repo.

@janosmiko We are back to using skopeo copy in regular use and have not seen this issue again recently, but all the repos and images we use are now using current formats.

For a time we added failover scripting that would try `skopeo copy` and if that failed use `crane copy` [crane](https://github.com/google/go-containerregistry/blob/main/cmd/crane/doc/crane.md)

Thank you @hobti01 !
I'll try it out.

> I faced the same issue, the credentials are valid. Changing the method from copy to sync fix the issue. (I think the issue is caused somehow related to image blobs that may already exist in the target registry, but the owner is another user. But this is just a wild guess.)

The only log that exists (that one quoted above) only shows attempts to access the destination repo, not any other repo. So, based on that limited information, I continue to think that the credentials are just not sufficient.

Maybe the credentials only allow writing, not reading, in the destination repo? Or there is some policy that allows HTTP GET, but not HTTP HEAD?

Either way, the server presumably knows why the operation was rejected.

In my case the error was a wrong destination path, hostname was ok, image name was ok, tag was ok. But the folder/organization was missing. So I claim the error message is misleading. Possibly that makes the difference between 'skopeo copy' and 'skopeo sync'.

Error: trying to reuse blob sha256:[some_sha] at destination: checking whether a blob sha256:[some_sha] exists in [repohost/image_name]: unauthorized: authentication required

With correct destination path all works.

> In my case the error was a wrong destination path, hostname was ok, image name was ok, tag was ok. But the folder/organization was missing. So I claim the error message is misleading.

Could you give a complete example of 1) command input 2) actual error message 3) expected error message, please?

> Possibly that makes the difference between 'skopeo copy' and 'skopeo sync'.

Yes, `skopeo copy` and `skopeo sync` do interpret user’s destination input differently; but the quoted error message “exists in …” should always report the actual destination repo that is failing. If it doesn’t, that’s a bug we’d really want to fix.
